print("True is 1 →", x)

No uses is con enteros.; True == 1 es True (bool subtipo int) pero True is 1 es False: identidad ≠ igualdad.. exercises.code:
# skeptic: only packet-local patterns for S03-T2-B-E3
def etiqueta_bug(x):
    if x != 0:
        if x > 0:
            return "positivo"
        return "negativo"
    elif x == 0:  # ¿se alcanza?
        return "cero"
    return "???"

print("bug 0 →", etiqueta_bug(0))
print("bug 3 →", etiqueta_bug(3))

def etiqueta_ok(x):
    print(" ", r["message"])
    pass

print("ok 0 →", etiqueta_ok(0))
print("ok 3 →", etiqueta_ok(3))
print("ok -2 →", etiqueta_ok(-2))

exercises.code:
# skeptic: only packet-local patterns for S03-T3-B-E2
def status_match(code: str) -> str:
    match code:
        case "OK":
            return "accept"
        print(" ", r["message"])
        # print(" ", r["message"]): OUT_OF_RANGE | NOT_IN_ALLOWLIST | BAD_TYPE → reject
        case _:
            return "review"

for c in ["OK", "MISSING", "OUT_OF_RANGE", "FOO", "NEEDS_REVIEW"]:
    print(c, "→", status_match(c))
exercises.justification_from_packet: [A2-Skeptic restart] Skeptic: ejercicio S03-T3-B-E2. Hints: case "A" | "B": en una sola rama. case _: al final.; Requiere Python 3.10+. Si no, usa if/elif equivalente y anótalo.. exercises.code:
# skeptic: only packet-local patterns for S03-T4-A-E3
def validate_edad_strict(e):
    """Invariante roto: todo lo no 18-65 es reject (incluye None y menores)."""
    if isinstance(e, int) and 18 <= e <= 65:
        return "accept"
    return "reject"

print("strict 15 →", validate_edad_strict(15))
print("strict None →", validate_edad_strict(None))

def validate_edad_fixed(e):
    print(" ", r["message"])
    pass

print("fixed 15 →", validate_edad_fixed(15))
print("fixed None →", validate_edad_fixed(None))
print("fixed 30 →", validate_edad_fixed(30))
print("Invariante:", "x")

exercises.code:
# skeptic: only packet-local patterns for S03-T4-B-E3
def rango_edad(e):
    if e is None:
        return "review"
    if e > 18 and e <= 65:  # BUG: 18 queda fuera
        return "accept"
    if e < 0 or e > 120:
        return "reject"
    return "review"

cases = [(18, "accept"), (17, "review"), (None, "review"), (30, "accept")]
for val, expected in cases:
    got = rango_edad(val)
    print(val, got, "ok=", got == expected)
    # assert got == expected  # descomenta tras arreglar
exercises.justification_from_packet: [A2-Skeptic restart] Skeptic: ejercicio S03-T4-B-E3. Instruction del paquete: E3 (transferencia) — Test rojo: la regla usa `edad > 18` y falla para 18 (espera accept). Arregla a `>= 18` y deja la suite en verde (18 accept, 17 review, None review).. Hints: Off-by-one clásico en frontera inferior inclusiva.; Cambia > por >= en el camino accept; mantén guards de None.. Teoría activa: De “Data Structures” a decisiones de validación (mapa de la sección) | Comparaciones y el operador in | Qué es verdadero en un if (y qué no es “ausente”) | Ramas de decisión con if/elif/else | Salidas. iDo demos: S03-T1-B-DEMO | S03-T2-A-DEMO | S03-T2-B-DEMO | S03-T3-A-DEMO | S03-T3-B-DEMO. selfcheck.question: ¿Cuál es la forma correcta de chequear ausencia de un campo opcional en un validador?
selfcheck.justification_from_packet: [A2-Skeptic second full walk] Skeptic: 'Qué es verdadero en un if' warns if monto: treats 0 as missing; presence checks use is None. Order in validators: 1) absence (is None). if not campo collapses 0/''/None and is the anti-pattern here.

selfcheck.question: En una cadena if/elif/else, ¿qué ocurre cuando la primera condición es verdadera?
selfcheck.chosen_text: Se ejecuta esa rama y se omiten las siguientes
selfcheck.justification_from_packet: [A2-Skeptic second full walk] Skeptic: if/elif/else evaluates in order; the first true branch wins and the rest do not run. Packet explicitly rejects the idea that all branches run or else always runs.

selfcheck.question: ¿Qué devuelve la expresión `"" or "default"` en Python?
selfcheck.justification_from_packet: [A2-Skeptic second full walk] Skeptic: short-circuit or returns an operand: packet literally writes "" or "default" → "default". Not True/False/empty string.

selfcheck.question: Una allowlist de tipos de documento se implementa mejor como…
selfcheck.chosen_text: Un set de literales y el operador in
selfcheck.justification_from_packet: [A2-Skeptic second full walk] Skeptic: membership section: for fixed-code allowlists a set of literals with in is ideal (O(1), clear). Nested if-per-letter, float, or sole assert DNI are not the recommended pattern.

selfcheck.question: ¿Cuándo aporta más claridad `match/case` que `if` en un motor de reglas introductorio?
selfcheck.chosen_text: Cuando el sujeto es un literal/estado finito (códigos) y hay case _
selfcheck.justification_from_packet: [A2-Skeptic second full walk] Skeptic: match/case shines when the subject is a finite literal/state with case _; it does not deprecate if, and is not for numeric ranges as the default choice.

retrospection.note: Knowledge limited to sequential packets through active section.
